refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (2) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (3) history log captures all updates with reason codes and approval chain; (4) traceability links each performance target to originating worksite input; (5) safety margins are explicitly derived and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, environmental conditions)
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish traceability links between each performance target and originating worksite input
CR-NGHE-005.5: Explicitly derive and document safety margins for all performance targets

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance derivation engine that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms to compute targets, stores baseline in a versioned configuration database, and logs all changes with audit trail
DP-B: Distributed performance derivation across vehicle and teleoperation modules, each computing targets locally and synchronizing via network, with eventual consistency on baseline
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by site manager with system-assisted validation and history tracking via spreadsheet export/import
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance derivation engine that ingests worksite mission parameters (terrain, payload, cycle count, environmental conditions), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins), stores baseline in a versioned configuration database with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval), and logs all changes with immutable audit trail. Engine exposes REST API for mission context ingestion and baseline query; integrates with telemetry for real-time mission parameter updates.

potential_sources: Coupling to Vehicle module: DP-A requires vehicle capability data (mass, power rating, efficiency curves) to compute targets. Mitigation: vehicle exposes capability interface; DP-A queries at initialization.
Coupling to Teleoperation module: DP-A may need operator input for mission context refinement. Mitigation: teleoperation provides mission context via Tasking interface.
Coupling to Safety Services: DP-A must coordinate safety margin derivation with safety policies. Mitigation: Safety Services provides policy constraints; DP-A applies them deterministically.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A is decoupled from execution logic (vehicle control, teleoperation commands). It is tightly coupled to mission context and safety policy inputs, which is appropriate for a derivation engine. Coupling is via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert), not internal dependencies.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, environmental conditions) at system initialization or via telemetry updates.
ASSUMPTION: Vehicle capability data (mass, power rating, efficiency curves) is available and stable during a mission.
ASSUMPTION: Safety policies and margin requirements are codified and can be expressed as deterministic constraints.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid targets. Mitigation: validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: If vehicle capability data is stale or incorrect, derived targets may be infeasible. Mitigation: periodic re-derivation and operator override capability.
RISK: If safety policies change mid-mission, audit trail may become inconsistent. Mitigation: policy versioning and explicit change approval workflow.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) derivation algorithm (e.g., physics-based model, lookup tables, machine learning); (2) baseline versioning scheme (semantic versioning, timestamp-based); (3) audit trail immutability guarantee (e.g., append-only log, cryptographic signing); (4) fallback behavior if mission context is incomplete.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the performance derivation engine, baseline management database, and audit trail. It integrates mission context from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, applies safety constraints from Safety Services, and exposes performance targets via Telemetry interface for consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, environmental conditions) from Teleoperation module via Tasking interface. Used as input to performance derivation engine.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety policy constraints and margin requirements from Safety Services module via SafetyAlert interface. Applied during performance target derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution publishes derived performance targets, baseline metadata, and history summaries to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Telemetry interface. Enables real-time monitoring and operator awareness.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for synchronization of baseline and audit trail across distributed nodes (e.g., site manager console, vehicle, tele-operator station). Ensures consistency and audit trail integrity.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of performance derivation and baseline management. It integrates mission context from Vehicle and Teleoperation, applies safety constraints, and publishes targets. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces are Tasking (in), SafetyAlert (in), Telemetry (out), Network Link (inout). High cohesion: all derivation logic, baseline storage, and audit trail are contained within this module.

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle control and telemetry, not performance requirement derivation. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would scatter derivation logic across vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling to vehicle-specific hardware and control loops. High diffusion risk: derivation engine would need to be replicated or tightly coupled to vehicle control, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and mission tasking, not performance requirement derivation. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would couple derivation logic to operator interface and network latency, creating fragility. High diffusion risk: derivation engine would be tightly coupled to teleoperation UI and command processing, violating separation of concerns.

Safety Services module is responsible for safety monitoring and policy enforcement, not performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services provides safety constraints as input to derivation, it should not own the derivation engine itself. Assigning DP-A to Safety Services would create circular dependency: Safety Services would need to derive targets, then validate them against its own policies. High diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner of DP-A (performance derivation engine, baseline management, audit trail). It is the top-level system integration boundary, naturally positioned to ingest mission context from Vehicle and Teleoperation, apply safety constraints from Safety Services, and publish targets to all consumers. Containment is high (95%) because all derivation logic, baseline storage, and audit trail are cohesive and isolated from vehicle control, teleoperation UI, and safety monitoring. Diffusion is minimal (5%) because external interfaces are well-defined (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) and do not require replication or tight coupling to other modules.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module' with too many responsibilities, it may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (e.g., PerformanceDerivationEngine, BaselineManager, AuditTrail) at the next decomposition level, with clear internal interfaces.

RISK: If mission context from Vehicle or Teleoperation is delayed or incomplete, derivation engine may produce stale or invalid targets. Mitigation: implement timeout and validation logic; publish uncertainty estimates with targets.

RISK: If Network Link fails, audit trail synchronization may be lost, compromising traceability. Mitigation: implement local audit trail with eventual consistency; use cryptographic signing to detect tampering.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment is sound. Recommend: (1) explicit specification of NGHE Solution sub-module boundaries (PerformanceDerivationEngine, BaselineManager, AuditTrail) at next decomposition level; (2) definition of internal interfaces between sub-modules; (3) specification of Network Link synchronization protocol (e.g., eventual consistency, conflict resolution); (4) specification of fallback behavior if mission context is incomplete or delayed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements from worksite mission context with full traceability and audit trail. Selected DP-A (centralized derivation engine with versioned baseline and immutable audit trail) directly addresses all FR elements: (1) derivation from mission context via deterministic algorithms, (2) recording with full metadata, (3) managed history with audit trail, (4) traceability links, (5) explicit safety margin derivation. DP-A is implementable, testable, and traceable to FR. No coupling issues identified that would prevent DP-A from satisfying FR.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-A requires mission context from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: interfaces are well-defined (Tasking, SafetyAlert); no replication of derivation logic across modules.
DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-A requires safety constraints from Safety Services. Mitigation: Safety Services provides constraints via SafetyAlert interface; no circular dependency or tight coupling.
DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-A publishes targets via Telemetry interface. Mitigation: Telemetry is a standard publish-subscribe interface; no tight coupling to consumers.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner of DP-A. Containment score is 95%, indicating high cohesion and minimal diffusion. All derivation logic, baseline storage, and audit trail are contained within NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined and do not require replication or tight coupling to other modules. No alternative module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) has higher containment or lower diffusion. IM assignment is sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible within NGHE Solution module. Test cases can be designed to: (1) inject worksite mission context via Tasking interface; (2) verify performance targets are computed correctly; (3) verify baseline is recorded with full metadata; (4) verify history log captures all updates; (5) verify traceability links are established; (6) verify safety margins are derived and documented. All verification can be performed at NGHE Solution level without requiring Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services integration.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear. NGHE Solution owns the performance derivation engine, baseline management database, and audit trail. Vehicle and Teleoperation modules provide mission context inputs; Safety Services provides safety constraints. All responsibilities are clearly allocated; no ambiguity or overlap.

recommended_revisions: Specify derivation algorithm (physics-based model, lookup tables, machine learning) at next decomposition level.
Specify baseline versioning scheme (semantic versioning, timestamp-based) and immutability guarantee (append-only log, cryptographic signing).
Specify fallback behavior if mission context is incomplete or delayed (e.g., conservative defaults, operator override).
Specify Network Link synchronization protocol for audit trail consistency (e.g., eventual consistency, conflict resolution).
Specify acceptance criteria for performance target accuracy (e.g., within 5% of actual cycle time, energy efficiency).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic; it contains five sub-FRs that require further decomposition. DP-A (centralized derivation engine) is sound and well-allocated to NGHE Solution. However, DP-A itself is complex and requires decomposition into sub-DPs for: (1) derivation algorithm, (2) baseline versioning and storage, (3) audit trail management, (4) traceability link establishment, (5) safety margin derivation. Each sub-DP requires its own IM assignment and gate review. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for sub-FRs CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR is well-structured and the DP-IM assignment is sound, but the complexity of the performance derivation system warrants further decomposition. The five sub-FRs are distinct and require separate design decisions: (1) CR-NGHE-005.1 (derivation algorithm) requires selection of algorithm type and parameters; (2) CR-NGHE-005.2 (baseline recording) requires specification of metadata schema and storage mechanism; (3) CR-NGHE-005.3 (history management) requires specification of versioning scheme and audit trail format; (4) CR-NGHE-005.4 (traceability) requires specification of link establishment and query mechanisms; (5) CR-NGHE-005.5 (safety margins) requires specification of margin calculation and validation. Proceeding to next level will clarify design and reduce risk.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, environmental conditions)
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, approval)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish traceability links between each performance target and originating worksite input
CR-NGHE-005.5: Explicitly derive and document safety margins for all performance targets

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain type, payload mass, cycle count, environmental conditions) at system initialization or via telemetry updates.
Vehicle capability data (mass, power rating, efficiency curves) is available and stable during a mission.
Safety policies and margin requirements are codified and can be expressed as deterministic constraints.
Performance targets can be derived deterministically from mission context and vehicle capabilities using physics-based models or lookup tables.
Baseline versioning and audit trail can be implemented using standard database and logging technologies.
Network Link is reliable and provides eventual consistency for distributed audit trail synchronization.
open_risks: RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid targets. Mitigation: validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: If vehicle capability data is stale or incorrect, derived targets may be infeasible. Mitigation: periodic re-derivation and operator override capability.
RISK: If safety policies change mid-mission, audit trail may become inconsistent. Mitigation: policy versioning and explicit change approval workflow.
RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module', it may become difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: decompose into sub-modules at next level.
RISK: If Network Link fails, audit trail synchronization may be lost. Mitigation: local audit trail with eventual consistency and cryptographic signing.
evidence_needed: Specification of derivation algorithm (physics-based model, lookup tables, machine learning) with validation data.
Specification of baseline versioning scheme and immutability guarantee (append-only log, cryptographic signing).
Specification of fallback behavior if mission context is incomplete or delayed.
Specification of Network Link synchronization protocol for audit trail consistency.
Acceptance criteria for performance target accuracy (e.g., within 5% of actual cycle time, energy efficiency).
Test cases for each sub-FR (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) with expected results.
Traceability matrix linking each sub-FR to acceptance criteria and test cases.
